Metadata.Coram: Chan Sek Keong CJ; Andrew Phang Boon Leong JA; V K Rajah JA

Party_Details.Name: Firstlink Energy Pte Ltd

Facts.Text: Firstlink Energy Pte Ltd was used as a wholly-owned subsidiary vehicle of Firstlink Investments Corporation Limited for its venture into the coal industry by November 2003.
Firstlink Energy Pte Ltd was the respondent in Civil Appeal No 114 of 2006 and Civil Appeal No 115 of 2006 involving coal-related investments with Creanovate Pte Ltd and PT Perdana Andalan Coal.
By November 2003, Firstlink Investments Corporation Limited acquired a 56.22% stake in Green Salt Group Ltd, which had investments in salt mine operations in China.
On 2004-07-01, Ngu Tieng Ung was appointed to the Executive Committee of Firstlink Investments Corporation Limited.

On 2004-08-19, Firstlink Energy Pte Ltd, Creanovate Pte Ltd and Tang Kok Heng entered into a joint venture agreement under which Creanovate and Tang were to supply coal exclusively to Firstlink Energy and act as its agents in coal purchases and sales, with Firstlink Energy providing financing and trading profits to be shared 40% to Firstlink Energy and 60% to Creanovate.

On 2004-08-24, the joint venture agreement dated 2004-08-19 between Firstlink Energy Pte Ltd, Creanovate Pte Ltd and Tang Kok Heng was presented to the board of directors of Firstlink Investments Corporation Limited.

Under clause 3 of the joint venture agreement, Firstlink Energy Pte Ltd was advance S$171,000 to Creanovate Pte Ltd to set up basic communications, living and transport infrastructure at the stockpile site prior to the start of the coal trading business.
On 2004-08-19, Firstlink Energy Pte Ltd disbursed S$30,000 to Creanovate Pte Ltd as part of the S$171,000 advance under the joint venture agreement.
On 2004-09-27, Firstlink Energy Pte Ltd disbursed S$35,000 to Creanovate Pte Ltd as part of the S$171,000 advance under the joint venture agreement.
On 2004-10-05, Firstlink Energy Pte Ltd disbursed S$15,000 to Creanovate Pte Ltd as part of the S$171,000 advance under the joint venture agreement.

On 2004-10-21, Firstlink Energy Pte Ltd disbursed S$90,000 to Creanovate Pte Ltd as part of the S$171,000 advance under the joint venture agreement, bringing the total disbursed under that clause to S$170,000.

On 2004-09-01, Tang Kok Heng requested from Firstlink Energy Pte Ltd an advance of S$30,000, stating that he needed the money urgently to settle some urgent outstanding matters, purportedly for coal mining.

On 2004-09-06, Creanovate Pte Ltd, through Tang Kok Heng, wrote to Firstlink Energy Pte Ltd inviting it to participate in a coal mining investment in Indonesia by taking up at least a 25% shareholding in a subsidiary of Creanovate and requested an advance of S$2,000,000 for this investment.

On 2004-09-07, Firstlink Energy Pte Ltd advanced S$940,000 to Creanovate Pte Ltd following a request from Tang Kok Heng to meet financial obligations related to a proposed coal mining investment in Indonesia.

On 2005-01-08, Firstlink Energy Pte Ltd, Creanovate Pte Ltd and PT Perdana Andalan Coal entered into a Subscription Agreement for a coal mining investment in Indonesia, under which Firstlink Energy was to advance S$2,000,000 to Creanovate, taking into account S$1,720,000 already advanced.

Under clause 2(2) of the Subscription Agreement dated 2005-01-08, sums advanced by Firstlink Energy Pte Ltd to Creanovate Pte Ltd were to be returned if specified conditions precedent regarding PT Perdana Andalan Coal's equity interests in PT Kencana Artha Buana and PT Senamas Energuido Mula were not met by 2005-02-22.

The Subscription Agreement dated 2005-01-08 provided that, satisfaction of conditions precedent, Firstlink Energy Pte Ltd would subscribe for S$3,500,000 worth of exchangeable bonds using the S$2,000,000 advance and an additional S$1,500,000 payable upon completion, which could be converted into a 30% equity interest in PT Perdana Andalan Coal.
On 2005-01-18, Firstlink Energy Pte Ltd advanced S$250,000 to Creanovate Pte Ltd pursuant to the Subscription Agreement framework.
On 2005-01-19, Firstlink Energy Pte Ltd advanced S$300,000 to Creanovate Pte Ltd pursuant to the Subscription Agreement framework.
On 2005-01-20, Firstlink Energy Pte Ltd advanced S$280,000 to Creanovate Pte Ltd pursuant to the Subscription Agreement framework.

On 2005-02-03, Firstlink Energy Pte Ltd advanced S$710,000 to Creanovate Pte Ltd, bringing the total advances to Creanovate to S$3,260,000, which exceeded the S$2,000,000 contemplated under the Subscription Agreement.

On 2005-02-21, Ngu Tieng Ung and Tang Kok Heng, in the name of Firstlink Energy Pte Ltd, requested S$1,000,000 from Firstlink Investments Corporation Limited purportedly for coal mining business in Indonesia and undertook that the sum would be repaid no later than two months from the date of the letter.
On 2005-02-22, Firstlink Investments Corporation Limited advanced S$1,000,000 following the 2005-02-21 request made in the name of Firstlink Energy Pte Ltd for coal mining business in Indonesia.
On 2005-03-07, Firstlink Energy Pte Ltd wrote to Creanovate Pte Ltd reminding it of its obligations under the Subscription Agreement dated 2005-01-08.
On 2005-03-09, Tang Kok Heng replied to Firstlink Energy Pte Ltd requesting an extension of the deadline under the Subscription Agreement to 2005-03-15.

On 2005-03-15, Tang Kok Heng wrote to Firstlink Energy Pte Ltd stating that he was attaching a legal opinion on the Indonesian coal mines structure and indicating that there were outstanding regulatory matters concerning PT Kencana Artha Buana's legal status and regulatory approval for changes in PT Senamas Energuido Mula's shareholders.

On 2005-04-05, the board of Firstlink Investments Corporation Limited resolved to cancel Firstlink Energy Pte Ltd's subscription of exchangeable bonds in Creanovate Pte Ltd and to convert the investment into a 51% direct equity interest in PT Perdana Andalan Coal to be held by Firstlink Coal Pte Ltd.

Minutes of the Firstlink Investments Corporation Limited board meeting on 2005-04-05 recorded that management had obtained a legal opinion from an Indonesian law firm stating that the corporate structure and joint venture arrangements were generally in place, but that 60% of PT Kencana Artha Buana shares were held by an individual on behalf of PT Perdana Andalan Coal and that Indonesian law did not recognise trusts.

Following the 2005-04-05 board decision, Firstlink Energy Pte Ltd, Creanovate Pte Ltd, PT Perdana Andalan Coal and Benhard Siagian entered into a Settlement Agreement under which the Subscription Agreement would be terminated and the sums advanced by Firstlink Energy to Creanovate would be settled by transferring 51% of the shares in PT Perdana Andalan Coal from Creanovate to Firstlink Energy, approval by the relevant Indonesian authority.

On 2005-05-18, the board of Firstlink Investments Corporation Limited recorded that, notwithstanding its earlier endorsement to enter into the Settlement Agreement, it considered that Ngu Tieng Ung was not authorised to sign because Creanovate Pte Ltd had misled Firstlink Energy Pte Ltd in view of a missing mine.

On 2005-04-18, the board of Firstlink Investments Corporation Limited decided that Tang Kok Heng would be given one week to propose a satisfactory scheme to find a replacement coal mine, failing which Firstlink Investments would terminate the Subscription Agreement and commence legal action to recover advances made to Creanovate Pte Ltd.
On 2005-05-05, Firstlink Investments Corporation Limited wrote to Ngu Tieng Ung and Tang Kok Heng requesting the return of the S$1,000,000 advance referred to in the 2005-02-21 letter.

By letter dated 2005-05-11, Creanovate Pte Ltd, through Tang Kok Heng, informed Firstlink Investments Corporation Limited that it would return S$700,000 by 2005-05-13 in relation to the S$1,000,000 advanced for coal trading purposes and that the remaining S$300,000 would be treated as balance of settlement for the subscription of exchangeable bonds in Creanovate.
On 2005-05-12, Creanovate Pte Ltd repaid S$90,000 to Firstlink Energy Pte Ltd.
On 2005-05-29, Ngu Tieng Ung returned a S$150,000 advance that he had taken on 2004-11-30 purportedly on behalf of Creanovate Pte Ltd.

On 2005-05-31, Darren Kee, on behalf of Firstlink Energy Pte Ltd, made a formal written demand to Creanovate Pte Ltd for the return of S$3,260,000 and stated that the Subscription Agreement was null and void as a result of a misrepresentation that Creanovate owned a coal mine owned by PT Senamas Energuido Mula.

At a Firstlink Investments Corporation Limited board meeting on 2005-06-03, Ngu Tieng Ung informed the board that he would discuss repayment of the S$1,000,000 loan to Creanovate Pte Ltd and address outstanding issues with Tang Kok Heng and report back to the board.

Party_Details.Issue: Whether advances and payments made by Firstlink Energy Pte Ltd and its parent to Creanovate Pte Ltd and related entities, in the context of coal mining and trading arrangements, were to be repaid or otherwise returned based on the terms of the joint venture, subscription, settlement and related funding arrangements.
Party_Details.Rule: Directors and company officers must act honestly and with reasonable diligence in managing company funds and must comply with statutory restrictions on company loans and financial assistance to directors and connected entities, including any conditions precedent and approval requirements set out in applicable companies legislation and corporate instruments.
Party_Details.Application: Firstlink Energy Pte Ltd and its parent advanced multiple sums to Creanovate Pte Ltd and related entities under the joint venture agreement, the Subscription Agreement, the Settlement Agreement and subsequent funding requests, with written terms addressing conditions precedent, repayment undertakings and alternative settlement by share transfers; the timing, amounts and documented purposes of these advances, together with subsequent board resolutions, demand letters and partial repayments, frame the question of how the statutory and contractual standards governing use and recovery of company funds apply to these transactions.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court accepted the trial judge's findings and dismissed Civil Appeal No 114 of 2006 and Civil Appeal No 115 of 2006 with costs. It agreed that Ngu Tieng Ung and Tang Kok Heng were liable to indemnify Firstlink Energy Pte Ltd for losses arising from their conduct as directors, characterising their use of funds as a misuse of company money and a breach of fiduciary and statutory duties under section 157 of the Companies Act. The court considered the evidential record, including banking records and the appellants' election to make a submission of no case to answer, and concluded that the funds advanced for coal-related ventures had been diverted for personal purposes and to stockbrokers. While the trial judge had additionally treated the advances as loans falling within sections 162 and 163 of the Companies Act, the Court of Appeal disagreed with that legal characterisation, holding that the term 'loan' in those sections should be confined to loans in the conventional sense and not extended to the broader range of transactions at issue. The court analysed the legislative history of sections 162 and 163, relevant UK and Australian authorities, and the evolution of related provisions on loans and quasi-loans, and concluded that the statutory loan prohibitions did not apply on the facts, but that liability was fully supported by breach of fiduciary and statutory director duties. The appeals were therefore dismissed, with the court affirming the monetary judgments against Creanovate Pte Ltd, Ngu Tieng Ung and Tang Kok Heng, subject only to its different interpretation of the scope of 'loan' under sections 162 and 163.

Metadata.Coram: Chan Sek Keong CJ; Andrew Phang Boon Leong JA; V K Rajah JA

Party_Details.Name: Creanovate Pte Ltd

Facts.Text: Creanovate Pte Ltd is a limited exempt private company incorporated in Singapore on 2004-07-27.
As at its incorporation on 2004-07-27, Tang Kok Heng was the majority and controlling shareholder of Creanovate Pte Ltd.

On 2004-08-19, Creanovate Pte Ltd and Tang Kok Heng entered into a joint venture agreement with Firstlink Energy Pte Ltd under which Creanovate and Tang were obligated to supply coal exclusively to Firstlink Energy and were authorised to act as its agents or representatives in the purchase and sale of coal.

Under the 2004-08-19 joint venture agreement, Creanovate Pte Ltd and Tang Kok Heng were to undertake the operational aspects of the coal trading business while Firstlink Energy Pte Ltd was to provide financing, with trading profits to be shared 60% to Creanovate and 40% to Firstlink Energy.

Pursuant to clause 3 of the joint venture agreement, Creanovate Pte Ltd received advances from Firstlink Energy Pte Ltd totalling S$170,000 between 2004-08-19 and 2004-10-21 to set up basic communications, living and transport infrastructure at a stockpile site prior to the start of the coal trading business.

On 2004-09-01, Tang Kok Heng, acting on behalf of Creanovate Pte Ltd, requested an advance of S$30,000 from Firstlink Energy Pte Ltd, stating that he needed the money urgently to settle urgent outstanding matters, purportedly for coal mining.

On 2004-09-06, Creanovate Pte Ltd, through Tang Kok Heng, wrote to Firstlink Energy Pte Ltd inviting it to participate in a coal mining investment in Indonesia by taking up at least a 25% shareholding in a Creanovate subsidiary and requested an advance of S$2,000,000 for this investment, including a request for S$940,000 by the next day to meet financial obligations.

On 2004-09-07, Creanovate Pte Ltd received S$940,000 from Firstlink Energy Pte Ltd following the 2004-09-06 request for funds to participate in a coal mining investment in Indonesia and to meet financial obligations.
On 2004-11-03, Creanovate Pte Ltd received S$500,000 from Firstlink Energy Pte Ltd as a further advance.
On 2004-11-17, Creanovate Pte Ltd received S$280,000 from Firstlink Energy Pte Ltd as a further advance.

On 2005-01-08, Creanovate Pte Ltd entered into a Subscription Agreement with Firstlink Energy Pte Ltd and PT Perdana Andalan Coal under which Creanovate was to receive a total advance of S$2,000,000 from Firstlink Energy, taking into account S$1,720,000 already advanced.

In connection with the Subscription Agreement dated 2005-01-08, Tang Kok Heng represented that PT Perdana Andalan Coal had a 60% equity interest in PT Kencana Artha Buana, which in turn had a 72.5% equity interest in PT Senamas Energuido Mula, and that PT Kencana Artha Buana and PT Senamas Energuido Mula had coal extraction concessions in southern Kalimantan.
On 2005-01-18, Creanovate Pte Ltd received S$250,000 from Firstlink Energy Pte Ltd as part of the Subscription Agreement funding.
On 2005-01-19, Creanovate Pte Ltd received S$300,000 from Firstlink Energy Pte Ltd as part of the Subscription Agreement funding.
On 2005-01-20, Creanovate Pte Ltd received S$280,000 from Firstlink Energy Pte Ltd as part of the Subscription Agreement funding.
On 2005-02-03, Creanovate Pte Ltd received S$710,000 from Firstlink Energy Pte Ltd, bringing total advances from Firstlink Energy to Creanovate to S$3,260,000.

On 2005-02-21, a letter issued in the name of Firstlink Energy Pte Ltd and signed by Ngu Tieng Ung and Tang Kok Heng requested S$1,000,000 from Firstlink Investments Corporation Limited purportedly for coal mining business in Indonesia, stating that the sum was needed to fund a shipment of coal to Paul Oil Co Ltd and undertaking repayment within two months.

Following a 2005-04-05 board decision of Firstlink Investments Corporation Limited, Creanovate Pte Ltd entered into a Settlement Agreement with Firstlink Energy Pte Ltd, PT Perdana Andalan Coal and Benhard Siagian under which the Subscription Agreement would be terminated and the sums advanced by Firstlink Energy to Creanovate would be settled by transferring 51% of the shares in PT Perdana Andalan Coal from Creanovate to Firstlink Energy, approval by the relevant Indonesian authority, failing which Creanovate had to refund the advance sum.

Under the Settlement Agreement entered into on or around 2005-04-05, Ngu Tieng Ung signed on behalf of Firstlink Energy Pte Ltd and Creanovate Pte Ltd agreed that the Subscription Agreement would be of no effect and that the advances would be settled by transfer of 51% of PT Perdana Andalan Coal shares, conditional on regulatory approval.

By letter dated 2005-05-11, Creanovate Pte Ltd, through Tang Kok Heng, informed Firstlink Investments Corporation Limited that it would return S$700,000 by 2005-05-13 in relation to the S$1,000,000 advanced for coal trading purposes and that the remaining S$300,000 would be treated as balance of settlement for the subscription of exchangeable bonds in Creanovate.
On 2005-05-12, Creanovate Pte Ltd repaid S$90,000 to Firstlink Energy Pte Ltd as part of the sums previously advanced.

On 2005-05-31, Creanovate Pte Ltd received a formal demand letter from Firstlink Energy Pte Ltd, signed by Darren Kee, requesting repayment of S$3,260,000 and stating that the Subscription Agreement was null and void due to a misrepresentation regarding ownership of a coal mine owned by PT Senamas Energuido Mula.

Party_Details.Issue: Whether Creanovate Pte Ltd was return or repay the S$3,260,000 and related sums advanced by Firstlink Energy Pte Ltd and its parent under the joint venture, subscription and settlement arrangements for coal mining and trading in Indonesia.
Party_Details.Rule: Where a company receives advances or funding under contractual arrangements that include conditions precedent, repayment provisions or settlement mechanisms, the recipient's obligation to return or account for those sums is determined by the agreed contractual terms and applicable company law standards governing use of corporate funds and transactions with related parties.
Party_Details.Application: Creanovate Pte Ltd entered into a joint venture agreement, a Subscription Agreement and a Settlement Agreement with Firstlink Energy Pte Ltd and related parties, under which it received multiple advances totalling S$3,260,000 and participated in a proposed coal mining investment structure; the written terms on conditions precedent, share transfers as settlement, and refund obligations, together with subsequent correspondence and partial repayments, provide the factual basis for assessing Creanovate's responsibility to return or account for the advances under the governing contractual and statutory framework.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The trial court granted judgment against Creanovate Pte Ltd for S$4.26 million, including S$3.26 million advanced under the coal investment arrangements and an additional S$1 million, and the Court of Appeal agreed with the trial judge's findings and decision on this aspect, dismissing Civil Appeal No 114 of 2006. The courts treated the advances as company funds that had been misused and concluded that Creanovate was responsible for repayment. The appellate court did not revisit the detailed reasoning on Creanovate's liability, instead expressly endorsing the trial judge's analysis in the grounds of decision, while clarifying that the statutory loan provisions in sections 162 and 163 of the Companies Act should be interpreted narrowly. The dismissal of the appeal left intact the monetary judgment against Creanovate for the sums claimed.

Metadata.Coram: Chan Sek Keong CJ; Andrew Phang Boon Leong JA; V K Rajah JA

Party_Details.Name: Ngu Tieng Ung

Facts.Text: By November 2003, Ngu Tieng Ung was a substantial shareholder of Asiacorp Development Ltd, which received approximately 13% of the enlarged share capital of Firstlink Investments Corporation Limited as consideration for Firstlink Investments acquiring a 56.22% stake in Green Salt Group Ltd.
At the time of the November 2003 transaction involving Green Salt Group Ltd, Ngu Tieng Ung was a director of a public listed company in Malaysia.
On 2004-07-01, Ngu Tieng Ung was appointed to the Executive Committee of Firstlink Investments Corporation Limited.
Around July 2004, Tang Kok Heng approached Ngu Tieng Ung with a business proposal for coal trading involving Firstlink Investments Corporation Limited and its subsidiaries.
On 2004-09-15, Ngu Tieng Ung was appointed as a director of Firstlink Energy Pte Ltd.

By mid-September 2004, after the appointments of Tang Kok Heng and Ngu Tieng Ung as directors of Firstlink Energy Pte Ltd, they managed Firstlink Energy's coal-related activities while other directors focused on other businesses of Firstlink Investments Corporation Limited.
By mid-September 2004, Ngu Tieng Ung and Tang Kok Heng took operational control of Firstlink Energy Pte Ltd's coal-related affairs, according to the account of executive director Darren Kee.
Topbound Pte Ltd was a company in which Ngu Tieng Ung was a controlling shareholder at the time of the banking transactions in early September 2004.

On 2004-09-08, proceeds from a S$550,000 cheque drawn on Creanovate Pte Ltd's account were used by Ngu Tieng Ung to purchase a S$500,000 cashier's order in favour of Topbound Pte Ltd, with the remaining amount taken in cash.
On 2004-11-30, Ngu Tieng Ung took an advance of S$150,000 purportedly on behalf of Creanovate Pte Ltd.
On 2005-05-29, Ngu Tieng Ung returned the S$150,000 advance that he had taken on 2004-11-30 purportedly on behalf of Creanovate Pte Ltd.

On 2005-02-21, Ngu Tieng Ung and Tang Kok Heng signed a letter in the name of Firstlink Energy Pte Ltd to Firstlink Investments Corporation Limited requesting S$1,000,000 purportedly for coal mining business in Indonesia and undertaking that the sum would be repaid within two months.

On 2005-02-22, Ngu Tieng Ung's brother, Ngu Tieng Chai, used funds to purchase a S$1,000,000 cashier's order in the name of Kim Eng Securities Pte Ltd, a stockbroking firm, following the S$1,000,000 advance requested on 2005-02-21.

On or around 2005-04-05, Ngu Tieng Ung signed the Settlement Agreement on behalf of Firstlink Energy Pte Ltd, under which the Subscription Agreement with Creanovate Pte Ltd and PT Perdana Andalan Coal would be terminated and the advances would be settled by transfer of 51% of PT Perdana Andalan Coal shares, regulatory approval.

At the Firstlink Investments Corporation Limited annual general meeting on 2005-04-28, Ngu Tieng Ung was absent and the board resolved that he was to confirm whether rumours of an investigation by the Securities Commission of Malaysia and an impounded passport were true.

At a Firstlink Investments Corporation Limited board meeting on 2005-05-06, it was noted from a Malaysian news article that on 2005-05-05 Ngu Tieng Ung had been charged by the Sessions Court in Malaysia for criminal of trust involving RM37 million related to a construction consultancy company about eight years earlier, and during a teleconference he tendered his resignation as CEO and managing director with immediate effect while requesting to remain as a non-independent non-executive director; the board accepted his resignation from the CEO and managing director roles and agreed that he would remain as a non-independent non-executive director.

At a Firstlink Investments Corporation Limited board meeting on 2005-06-03, Ngu Tieng Ung informed the board that he would discuss repayment of the S$1,000,000 loan to Creanovate Pte Ltd and address outstanding issues with Tang Kok Heng and report back to the board.

Party_Details.Issue: Whether advances and payments connected with transactions involving Creanovate Pte Ltd and related entities, which were authorised or facilitated by Ngu Tieng Ung in his roles within the Firstlink group, should be repaid or accounted for by him personally based on his conduct in relation to company funds.
Party_Details.Rule: Individuals who hold positions within a corporate group and participate in authorising or arranging the use of company funds are general company law standards requiring honest conduct, reasonable diligence and proper use of corporate assets, and may be account for losses where their conduct in relation to company funds does not comply with those standards.
Party_Details.Application: Ngu Tieng Ung held senior roles within the Firstlink group, became a director of Firstlink Energy Pte Ltd, participated in coal-related ventures with Creanovate Pte Ltd, signed funding requests and the Settlement Agreement, received and later repaid a S$150,000 advance, and was associated with a S$500,000 cashier's order purchased for Topbound Pte Ltd and a S$1,000,000 cashier's order purchased by his brother; these events form the factual basis for assessing whether his handling and authorisation of company funds align with the applicable standards for corporate officers and directors.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The trial judge granted judgment against Ngu Tieng Ung for S$4.26 million less S$940,000, and the Court of Appeal upheld this outcome in Civil Appeal No 115 of 2006. The appellate court agreed that Ngu, as a director of Firstlink Energy Pte Ltd, was responsible for misuse of company funds advanced to Creanovate Pte Ltd and related entities, and that he was required to indemnify Firstlink Energy for the resulting losses. The court relied on banking records showing diversions of funds, including payments to Topbound Pte Ltd and stockbrokers, and noted that Ngu elected to make a submission of no case to answer, leaving the respondent's evidence unrebutted. The court characterised his conduct as a clear misuse of company funds and a breach of fiduciary and statutory duties under section 157 of the Companies Act. It rejected the trial judge's reliance on sections 162 and 163 of the Companies Act to treat the advances as prohibited loans, holding instead that the term 'loan' in those sections should be confined to conventional loans and that the facts were more appropriately addressed under fiduciary and statutory director duties. The appeal was dismissed and Ngu's personal liability for the shortfall was affirmed.

Metadata.Coram: Chan Sek Keong CJ; Andrew Phang Boon Leong JA; V K Rajah JA

Party_Details.Name: Tang Kok Heng

Facts.Text: From its incorporation on 2004-07-27, Tang Kok Heng was the majority and controlling shareholder of Creanovate Pte Ltd.
Around July 2004, Tang Kok Heng approached Ngu Tieng Ung with a business proposal for coal trading involving Firstlink Investments Corporation Limited and its subsidiaries.
On 2004-08-10, Tang Kok Heng made a presentation to Darren Kee, the executive director of Firstlink Investments Corporation Limited, regarding a business proposal for coal trading.

On 2004-08-19, Tang Kok Heng and Creanovate Pte Ltd entered into a joint venture agreement with Firstlink Energy Pte Ltd under which Tang and Creanovate were obligated to supply coal exclusively to Firstlink Energy and were authorised to act as its agents or representatives in the purchase and sale of coal.

Under the 2004-08-19 joint venture agreement, Tang Kok Heng and Creanovate Pte Ltd were to undertake the operational aspects of the coal trading business while Firstlink Energy Pte Ltd was to provide financing, with trading profits to be shared 60% to Creanovate and 40% to Firstlink Energy.

On 2004-08-19, Firstlink Energy Pte Ltd disbursed S$30,000 to Creanovate Pte Ltd under clause 3 of the joint venture agreement to set up basic communications, living and transport infrastructure at the stockpile site.
On 2004-09-27, Firstlink Energy Pte Ltd disbursed S$35,000 to Creanovate Pte Ltd under clause 3 of the joint venture agreement.
On 2004-10-05, Firstlink Energy Pte Ltd disbursed S$15,000 to Creanovate Pte Ltd under clause 3 of the joint venture agreement.
On 2004-10-21, Firstlink Energy Pte Ltd disbursed S$90,000 to Creanovate Pte Ltd under clause 3 of the joint venture agreement, bringing the total disbursed under that clause to S$170,000.

On 2004-09-01, Tang Kok Heng requested an advance of S$30,000 from Firstlink Energy Pte Ltd, stating that he needed the money urgently to settle urgent outstanding matters, purportedly for coal mining.

On 2004-09-06, Creanovate Pte Ltd, through Tang Kok Heng, wrote to Firstlink Energy Pte Ltd inviting it to participate in a coal mining investment in Indonesia by taking up at least a 25% shareholding in a Creanovate subsidiary and requested an advance of S$2,000,000, including a request for S$940,000 by the next day to meet financial obligations.

On 2004-09-07, Creanovate Pte Ltd received S$940,000 from Firstlink Energy Pte Ltd following Tang Kok Heng's request for funds to participate in a coal mining investment in Indonesia and to meet financial obligations.

On 2004-09-07, Tang Kok Heng went to OCBC Bank to encash two cheques drawn on Creanovate Pte Ltd's account for S$500,000 and S$250,000, and the proceeds were used to purchase cashier's orders for S$500,000 in favour of OCBC Securities Pte Ltd and S$250,000 in favour of UOB Kay Hian Pte Ltd, with Creanovate Pte Ltd named as the applicant.

On 2004-09-08, two cashier's orders previously purchased using Creanovate Pte Ltd funds were re-deposited into Creanovate's account, after which Tang Kok Heng encashed two cheques for S$550,000 and S$200,000; the proceeds from the S$550,000 cheque were used by Ngu Tieng Ung to purchase a S$500,000 cashier's order in favour of Topbound Pte Ltd, with the remaining amounts taken in cash.
On 2004-09-09, Tang Kok Heng was appointed as a director of Firstlink Energy Pte Ltd.
On 2004-11-03, Firstlink Energy Pte Ltd advanced S$500,000 to Creanovate Pte Ltd, and banking records showed that this sum was withdrawn in cash by Tang Kok Heng on the same day.
On 2004-11-17, Firstlink Energy Pte Ltd advanced S$280,000 to Creanovate Pte Ltd, and banking records showed that this sum was withdrawn in cash by Tang Kok Heng on the same day.

On 2005-01-08, Tang Kok Heng participated in the Subscription Agreement between Firstlink Energy Pte Ltd, Creanovate Pte Ltd and PT Perdana Andalan Coal, under which Firstlink Energy was to advance S$2,000,000 to Creanovate, taking into account S$1,720,000 already advanced.

In connection with the Subscription Agreement dated 2005-01-08, Tang Kok Heng represented that PT Perdana Andalan Coal held a 60% equity interest in PT Kencana Artha Buana, which in turn held a 72.5% equity interest in PT Senamas Energuido Mula, and that PT Kencana Artha Buana and PT Senamas Energuido Mula had coal extraction concessions in southern Kalimantan.
On 2005-01-18, Firstlink Energy Pte Ltd advanced S$250,000 to Creanovate Pte Ltd as part of the Subscription Agreement funding.

On 2005-01-19, Firstlink Energy Pte Ltd advanced S$300,000 to Creanovate Pte Ltd, and banking records showed that Tang Kok Heng's wife, Lim Lee Foon, encashed three cheques drawn on Creanovate's account and received S$355,000 in cash on that date.
On 2005-01-19, Lim Lee Foon, the wife of Tang Kok Heng, encashed three separate cheques drawn on Creanovate Pte Ltd's bank account and received S$355,000 in cash.
On 2005-01-20, Firstlink Energy Pte Ltd advanced S$280,000 to Creanovate Pte Ltd as part of the Subscription Agreement funding.
On 2005-02-03, Firstlink Energy Pte Ltd advanced S$710,000 to Creanovate Pte Ltd, and banking records showed that this sum was withdrawn in cash by Tang Kok Heng on the same day.

On 2005-02-21, Tang Kok Heng and Ngu Tieng Ung signed a letter in the name of Firstlink Energy Pte Ltd to Firstlink Investments Corporation Limited requesting S$1,000,000 purportedly for coal mining business in Indonesia and undertaking that the sum would be repaid within two months.
On 2005-03-07, Firstlink Energy Pte Ltd wrote to Creanovate Pte Ltd reminding it of its obligations under the Subscription Agreement dated 2005-01-08.
On 2005-03-09, Tang Kok Heng replied to Firstlink Energy Pte Ltd requesting an extension of the deadline under the Subscription Agreement to 2005-03-15.

On 2005-03-15, Tang Kok Heng wrote to Firstlink Energy Pte Ltd stating that he was attaching a legal opinion on the Indonesian coal mines structure and indicating that there were outstanding regulatory matters concerning PT Kencana Artha Buana's legal status and regulatory approval for changes in PT Senamas Energuido Mula's shareholders.

On or around 2005-04-05, Tang Kok Heng, as a principal of Creanovate Pte Ltd, participated in the Settlement Agreement with Firstlink Energy Pte Ltd, PT Perdana Andalan Coal and Benhard Siagian, under which the Subscription Agreement would be terminated and the advances would be settled by transfer of 51% of PT Perdana Andalan Coal shares, regulatory approval, failing which Creanovate had to refund the advance sum.
On 2005-04-19, Tang Kok Heng was dismissed as a director of Firstlink Energy Pte Ltd and resigned on the same date.

At a Firstlink Investments Corporation Limited board meeting on 2005-05-18, the board decided that Tang Kok Heng would be given one week to propose a satisfactory scheme to find a replacement coal mine, failing which the Subscription Agreement would be terminated and legal action would be commenced to recover advances made to Creanovate Pte Ltd.
On 2005-05-05, Firstlink Investments Corporation Limited wrote to Ngu Tieng Ung and Tang Kok Heng requesting the return of the S$1,000,000 advance referred to in the 2005-02-21 letter.

By letter dated 2005-05-11, Creanovate Pte Ltd, through Tang Kok Heng, informed Firstlink Investments Corporation Limited that it would return S$700,000 by 2005-05-13 in relation to the S$1,000,000 advanced for coal trading purposes and that the remaining S$300,000 would be treated as balance of settlement for the subscription of exchangeable bonds in Creanovate.
On 2005-05-12, Creanovate Pte Ltd repaid S$90,000 to Firstlink Energy Pte Ltd as part of the sums previously advanced.

Party_Details.Issue: Whether advances and payments connected with transactions involving Creanovate Pte Ltd and related entities, which were requested, received or withdrawn by Tang Kok Heng in his roles with Creanovate and Firstlink Energy Pte Ltd, should be repaid or accounted for by him personally based on his conduct in relation to company funds.
Party_Details.Rule: Individuals who control or manage companies and participate in requesting, receiving or withdrawing company funds are general company law standards requiring honest conduct, reasonable diligence and proper use of corporate assets, and may be account for losses where their handling of company funds does not comply with those standards.
Party_Details.Application: Tang Kok Heng was the majority shareholder of Creanovate Pte Ltd, a director of Firstlink Energy Pte Ltd, and a key participant in the coal trading and mining arrangements; he requested and received multiple advances from Firstlink Energy, withdrew large sums in cash from Creanovate's account, signed funding requests and correspondence, and was involved in the Settlement Agreement and subsequent repayment proposals, providing the factual basis for assessing whether his conduct in relation to company funds aligns with the applicable standards for company controllers and directors.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The trial judge granted judgment against Tang Kok Heng for S$4.26 million less S$940,000, and the Court of Appeal upheld this outcome in Civil Appeal No 115 of 2006. The appellate court agreed that Tang, as a director of Firstlink Energy Pte Ltd and the controlling shareholder of Creanovate Pte Ltd, was responsible for misuse of company funds advanced to Creanovate and related entities, and that he was required to indemnify Firstlink Energy for the resulting losses. The court relied on detailed banking records showing that funds advanced for coal-related ventures were withdrawn in cash or channelled to stockbrokers and related entities, and noted that Tang elected to make a submission of no case to answer, leaving the respondent's evidence unrebutted. The court characterised his conduct as a clear misuse of company funds and a breach of fiduciary and statutory duties under section 157 of the Companies Act. It disagreed with the trial judge's use of sections 162 and 163 of the Companies Act to treat the advances as prohibited loans, holding instead that the term 'loan' in those sections should be confined to conventional loans and that Tang's liability was properly grounded in fiduciary and statutory director duties. The appeal was dismissed and Tang's personal liability for the shortfall was affirmed.
